title: Mid session 2901 T225 C
body:
NAME OF CANDIDATE: STUDENT NUMBER:

THE UNIVERSITY OF NEW SOUTH WALES

SCHOOL OF MATHEMATICS AND STATISTICS

Term 2, 2025

MATH2901 Higher Theory of Statistics Mid-session test C

(1) TIME ALLOWED – 50 minutes

(2) TOTAL NUMBER OF QUESTIONS – 3

(3) ANSWER ALL QUESTIONS

(4) THE QUESTIONS ARE OF EQUAL VALUE

(5) THIS PAPER MAY NOT BE RETAINED BY THE CANDIDATE

(6) Write your answers on the test paper in space provided. You may use your own calculators. You are allowed a one-sided (A4) handwritten formula sheet of your own. Ask if you need more paper.

All answers must be written in ink. Except where they are expressly required pencils may only be used for drawing, sketching or graphical work.  Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 2

1. [10 marks] Let N ∼ Bin(8; 1/4) be the number of customers who enter a store each hour. Each customer i independently spends an amount Yi that follows an exponential distribution with parameter λ = 1/20 (so the expected spending per customer is $20).

a) [2 marks] During an hour when n customers entered the store, find the expected total amount spent by all customers.

b) [5 marks] Find the expected total amount spent by customers during a random hour. (Hint: use the Law of Iterated Expectations)

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 3

c) [3 marks] Suppose you learn that a particular customer spent more than $50. Given this information, what is the expected amount that this customer spent? (Hint: Use the memoryless property of the exponential distribution.)

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 4

2. [10 marks] Let (X1 , X2 ) be a continuous bivariate vector with joint density

fX1 X2 (x1 , x2 ) = cx1 1I{0≤x1 ≤1,x1 ≤x2 ≤1}

for a constant c > 0.

a) [3 marks] Determine the normalisation constant c.

b) [2 marks] Are X1 and X2 independent? Justify.

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 5

c) [2 marks] Find the marginal density of X1 .

d) [3 marks] Find the conditional density fX2 |X1 (x2 |x1 ). Name this distribution (i.e., the conditional distribution of X2 given X1 = x1 ), and deduce the conditional expectation E(X2 |X1 = x1 ).

Term 2, 2025 MATH2901 Page 6

3. [10 marks] A quality control inspector uses a scale to measure the weight of products. The scale readings are normally distributed with the true weight as the mean and a standard deviation of 0.05 kg due to measurement error.

a) [4 marks] If a product has a true weight of w kg, find the probability that a single measurement will be within 0.08 kg of the true weight.

b) [6 marks] If the same product is measured n = 25 times, find the probability that the average of the 25 measurements is within 0.02 kg of the true weight.

(Hint: Φ(0.02) = 0.5080 Φ(0.08) = 0.5319, Φ(1.6) = 0.9452, Φ(1.96) = 0.975, Φ(2) = 0.9772, Φ(2.5) = 0.9938)
